name: Poodle
description: Despite its glamorous reputation, the Poodle was originally bred in Germany as a skilled water retriever, and its iconic show clips were designed to protect joints while swimming in cold water. Consistently ranked among the top two most intelligent dog breeds, Poodles come in Standard, Miniature, and Toy sizes and excel at virtually every canine sport. Their low-shedding coat makes them a popular choice for allergy sufferers, though regular professional grooming every six to eight weeks is essential to prevent matting.

conditions_detail.name: Hip Dysplasia
conditions_detail.description: Hip dysplasia is a genetic condition where the hip joint doesn't develop properly, causing the ball and socket to not fit together correctly. This leads to joint deterioration, pain, and arthritis over time.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Treatment ranges from weight management and physical therapy for mild cases to total hip replacement for severe cases. NSAIDs and joint supplements help manage pain.
prevention_tips: Screen breeding stock with hip scoring

conditions_detail.name: Progressive Retinal Atrophy (PRA)
conditions_detail.description: PRA is a group of hereditary diseases causing progressive degeneration of the photoreceptor cells in the retina. It leads to night blindness first, followed by complete blindness. There is no treatment, but DNA tests exist for many forms.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: There is no effective treatment or cure for PRA. Management focuses on adapting the dog's environment to maintain quality of life as vision deteriorates.
prevention_tips: DNA test breeding stock before mating
Only breed clear or carrier-to-clear pairings

conditions_detail.name: Epilepsy
conditions_detail.description: Epilepsy is the most common neurological disorder in dogs, characterised by recurrent seizures. Idiopathic (genetic) epilepsy is the most frequent form in young to middle-aged dogs with no underlying structural brain lesion.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Anticonvulsant drugs (phenobarbital, potassium bromide, levetiracetam) control seizures in the majority of cases. Treatment is lifelong. Cluster seizures and status epilepticus require emergency treatment.
prevention_tips: DNA testing available for several breeds
Avoid breeding affected dogs with idiopathic epilepsy

conditions_detail.name: Sebaceous Adenitis
conditions_detail.description: Sebaceous adenitis is an inflammatory skin disease in which the sebaceous glands are selectively destroyed by immune-mediated inflammation. This results in follicular scaling, matting, and hair loss with a characteristic musty odour.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Intensive topical oil treatments (baby oil soaks), special shampoos, essential fatty acid supplements, and immunomodulatory drugs (cyclosporine) manage the condition. It cannot be cured but can be well-controlled.
prevention_tips: DNA testing under development for some breeds
Skin biopsy before breeding to exclude subclinical disease

conditions_detail.name: Addison's Disease (Hypoadrenocorticism)
conditions_detail.description: Addison's disease is caused by destruction of the adrenal cortex, leading to deficiency of glucocorticoids and mineralocorticoids. It can present as acute life-threatening 'Addisonian crisis' or with vague chronic signs that mimic many other conditions.
conditions_detail.treatment_overview: Acute crisis requires emergency IV fluids, dextrose, and corticosteroids. Maintenance involves lifelong monthly DOCP injections (mineralocorticoid) and daily prednisolone (glucocorticoid).
prevention_tips: Genetic research ongoing for some breeds
